name: Garlic Steak Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 3 large Green peppers, cut into 1/2-inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the meat against the grain into 1/2-inch strips (easier if partially frozen).
2. Make a marinade by combining soy sauce, bouillon, ginger, mashed garlic, and sherry. Marinate steak for 2-12 hours in the refrigerator.
3. Dry the meat on paper towels.
4. Heat peanut oil in a wok until golden brown.
5. Remove chopped garlic and sauté until golden brown. Set aside.
6. Add the meat to the hot oil and sauté until brown, adding a dash of sherry or sesame oil while browning.
7. Add reserved marinade and boiling water. Simmer for 45 minutes or less time, if desired.
8. When the meat is tender, remove and keep warm in the oven.
9. Pour the remaining marinade into a separate pan and add cornstarch. Add the remaining boiling water, if needed. Simmer until thick.
10. Stir-fry green peppers and sliced water chestnuts in the liquid remaining in the wok.
11. Add meat and marinade gravy. Sprinkle with a dash of sesame oil to taste.
12. Serve over hot boiled rice.
